name: Paper Chaser MCP
description_for_model: Use Paper Chaser MCP as a trust-graded evidence tool. Start with research for discovery, literature review, known-item recovery, citation repair, and regulatory routing; use follow_up_research for one grounded question over a saved searchSessionId; use resolve_reference for citations, DOI strings, arXiv IDs, URLs, and regulatory references; use inspect_source to inspect one sourceId from research; use get_runtime_status when behavior differs across environments. If you intentionally switch this sample to mcp-tools.full.json and run with PAPER_CHASER_TOOL_PROFILE=expert, you may also use raw and provider-specific expert tools such as search_papers_smart, search_federal_register, get_cfr_text, and explicit ScholarAPI/OpenAlex paths.
conversation_starters: Research retrieval-augmented generation for coding agents and show only the most trustworthy findings.
Resolve this incomplete reference and tell me the safest next step plus any alternatives.
Research the regulatory history of California condor under 50 CFR 17.95 and call out any evidence gaps.
Use the searchSessionId from my last research run to answer one grounded follow-up question.
Inspect one source from my last research run and tell me whether it is safe to rely on.
Research the rulemaking and evidence trail behind a CFR citation and point me to the most trustworthy primary sources.
Research recent papers on graphene and separate trustworthy findings from weaker leads.
notes: This sample points at mcp-tools.core.json and matches the guided-first public contract with PAPER_CHASER_TOOL_PROFILE=guided.
If you need the broader operator surface, switch to mcp-tools.full.json with PAPER_CHASER_TOOL_PROFILE=expert.
Resources are surfaced from tool outputs via resourceUris and searchSessionId handles.
If research abstains or asks for clarification, treat that as a real result rather than filling in unsupported synthesis.

For broken references, start with resolve_reference; for clearly regulatory references, use research first and pivot into the Federal Register/CFR expert tools only when intentionally running the expert package.
The server remains universal; these assets are packaging guidance, not a separate build.
